Heading.TitleText: Regulations Relieving Special Duty on Certain Tobacco Products

Subsection.Text: The Regulations Relieving Special Duty on Certain Tobacco Products are repealed.
Subsection (1) comes into force on the first day of the month after the month in which this Act receives royal assent.

Heading.TitleText: Stamping and Marking of Tobacco, Cannabis and Vaping Products Regulations

Subsection.Text: Section 4.01 of the Stamping and Marking of Tobacco, Cannabis and Vaping Products Regulations is amended by renumbering subsection (2) as subsection (3) and by adding the following after subsection (1):

If the Minister holds, at any time in a calendar month, security that a person has provided under subsection 25.1(3) of the Act and if the person is not a tobacco licensee throughout the calendar month, the person must file with the Minister an information return for the calendar month in respect of the possession and use of any tobacco excise stamps issued to the person.
Subsection (1) comes into force on the first day of the month after the month in which this Act receives royal assent.

Heading.TitleText: Regulations Respecting Prescribed Brands of Manufactured Tobacco and Prescribed Cigarettes

Subsection.Text: The Regulations Respecting Prescribed Brands of Manufactured Tobacco and Prescribed Cigarettes are repealed.
Subsection (1) comes into force on the first day of the month after the month in which this Act receives royal assent.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 3
Heading.TitleText: Underused Housing Tax Act and Underused Housing Tax Regulations

Subsection.Text: The definition excluded owner in section 2 of the Underused Housing Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: excluded owner of a residential property for a calendar year means a person (other than a prescribed person) that, on December 31 of the calendar year, is an owner of the residential property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the person’s capacity as a trustee of a trust that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a specified Canadian trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a mutual fund trust as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a real estate investment trust as defined in subsection 122.1(1) of the Income Tax Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a SIFT trust as defined in subsection 122.1(1) of the Income Tax Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the person’s capacity as a partner of a partnership that is a specified Canadian partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in a capacity other than as a trustee of a trust or partner of a partnership if the person is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an individual who is a citizen or permanent resident,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a specified Canadian corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation incorporated or continued under the laws of Canada or a province whose shares are listed on a stock exchange in Canada for which a designation under section 262 of the Income Tax Act is in effect,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered charity as defined in subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a cooperative housing corporation, a hospital authority, a municipality, a public college, a school authority, or a university, as those terms are defined in subsection 123(1) of the Excise Tax Act, or a para-municipal organization as defined in section 1 of Part VI of Schedule V to that Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: an Indigenous governing body as defined in section 2 of the Department of Indigenous Services Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: His Majesty in right of Canada or a province or an agent of His Majesty in right of Canada or a province,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation incorporated or continued under the laws of Canada or a province, all or substantially all of the shares of which are owned or controlled by

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a trust referred to in any of subparagraphs (a)(ii) to (iv),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a corporation referred to in subparagraph (iii), or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: any combination of corporations or trusts referred to in clauses (A) and (B); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the person is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an individual who is a citizen or permanent resident and an owner of the residential property as a personal representative in respect of a deceased individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a prescribed person. (propriétaire exclu)

Subsection.Text: The definition ownership percentage in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: ownership percentage, in respect of a particular person that is an owner of a residential property for a calendar year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if prescribed circumstances exist, a prescribed percentage;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraph (a) does not apply, if section 4.1 applies in respect of the particular person, if the land registration system or other similar system applicable where the residential property is located does not separately indicate a percentage of the ownership in respect of the residential property for each capacity in respect of which this Act applies to the particular person as a separate person under section 4.1, if the particular person indicates a particular percentage in their return for the residential property for the calendar year and if the Minister is satisfied that the particular percentage reasonably reflects the particular person’s percentage of the ownership in respect of the residential property, the particular percentage; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if paragraphs (a) and (b) do not apply,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the land registration system or other similar system applicable where the residential property is located indicates that the particular person holds a percentage of the ownership in respect of the residential property, that percentage,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the land registration system or other similar system applicable where the residential property is located indicates that a group of persons that includes the particular person holds a percentage of the ownership in respect of the residential property, that percentage divided by the total number of persons in the group, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in any other case, the percentage determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the sum of all percentages, each of which is a percentage of the ownership in respect of the residential property that is indicated in the land registration system or other similar system applicable where the residential property is located as being held by another person or a group of persons, and

is the total number of persons, determined without reference to section 4.1, each of which is an owner of the residential property in respect of which no percentage of the ownership in respect of the residential property is indicated in the land registration system or other similar system applicable where the residential property is located. (pourcentage de propriété)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (e) of the definition owner in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a person that gives continuous possession of the land on which the residential property is situated to another person referred to in paragraph (b) or (c), or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition specified Canadian partnership in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership, each member of which is, on December 31 of the calendar year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person referred to in paragraph (c) of the definition excluded owner,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust referred to in paragraph (a) of that definition, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a specified Canadian partnership; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition specified Canadian trust in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a trust under which each beneficiary having a beneficial interest in the residential property is, on December 31 of the calendar year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a person referred to in paragraph (c) of the definition excluded owner,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust referred to in paragraph (a) of that definition, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a specified Canadian partnership; or

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1), (4) and (5) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2023.
Subsections (2) and (3) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 4:

Section.Text: If a person is an owner of a residential property in the person’s capacity as a partner of a partnership or a trustee of a trust, this Act applies to the person as if the person were a separate person in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each partnership for which the person is an owner of the residential property in the person’s capacity as a partner of the partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each trust for which the person is an owner of the residential property in the person’s capacity as a trustee of the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all other capacities in which the person is an owner of the residential property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
The portion of subsection 6(3) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

Subject to this Act, every person that, on December 31 of a calendar year, is an owner of a residential property (other than an excluded owner of the residential property) must pay to His Majesty in right of Canada tax in respect of the residential property for the calendar year in the amount determined by the formula
Paragraphs 6(7)(a) and (b) of the Act are repealed.
Paragraph 6(8)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a child, of the individual or the individual’s spouse or common-law partner, who occupies the dwelling unit for the purpose of authorized study at a designated learning institution as defined in section 211.1 of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2023.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.

Section.Text: Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person that, on December 31 of a calendar year, is an owner of a residential property (other than an excluded owner of the residential property) is required to file a return for the residential property for the calendar year.
The portion of the definition confidential information in subsection 32(1) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: but does not include information that does not directly or indirectly reveal the identity of the person to whom it relates and, for the purposes of applying subsections (3), (13) and (14) to a representative of a government entity that is not an official, includes only the information described in paragraph 211(6)(j) of the Excise Act, 2001. (renseignement confidentiel)

Subsection.Text: Subsections 32(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Subsections 211(6) and (6.1) of the Excise Act, 2001 apply in this Act, with any modifications that the circumstances require, as if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the references in those subsections to the Excise Act, 2001 were references to this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: confidential information for the purposes of this Act were confidential information for the purposes of the Excise Act, 2001;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an authorized person for the purposes of this Act were an authorized person for the purposes of the Excise Act, 2001; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a business number for the purposes of this Act were a business number for the purposes of the Excise Act, 2001.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Paragraph 47(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $1,000 if the person is an individual or $2,000 if the person is not an individual; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 47(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a person fails to file a return in respect of a residential property for a particular calendar year by December 31 of the following calendar year, for the purpose of calculating the total referred to in paragraph (1)(b), the tax calculated under section 6 in respect of the residential property for the particular calendar year is to be determined without reference to paragraphs 6(7)(c) to (f) and subsections 6(8) and (9) of this Act and paragraph 2(3)(a) of the Underused Housing Tax Regulations.
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2022.
Subsection (2) applies in respect of returns for the 2023 and subsequent calendar years.
The portion of subsection 62(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (3), the authorized person may, at all reasonable times, for any purpose related to the administration or enforcement of this Act
Subsection 62(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraph (b) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: require any person to give the authorized person all reasonable assistance, to answer all proper questions relating to the administration or enforcement of this Act and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to attend with the authorized person at a place designated by the authorized person, or by video-conference or by another form of electronic communication, and to answer the questions orally, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to answer the questions in writing, in any form specified by the authorized person; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: require any person to give the authorized person all reasonable assistance with anything the authorized person is authorized to do under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 62(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If any place referred to in subsection (2) is a dwelling-house, the authorized person may not enter that dwelling-house without the consent of the occupant, except under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (4).
Paragraph 62(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there are reasonable grounds to believe that the dwelling-house is a place referred to in subsection (2);

Subsection.Text: Section 80 of the Act is amended by adding the following before subsection (1):
In this section, transaction includes an arrangement or event.
Section 80 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
For the purposes of this section, if a person transfers property to another person as part of a transaction or series of transactions, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is deemed to not be dealing at arm’s length with the transferee at the time of the transfer of the property if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the transferor and the transferee do not deal at arm’s length at any time during the period beginning immediately prior to the transaction or series of transactions and ending immediately after the transaction or series of transactions, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the transferee and the transferor under this section for an amount payable under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that the transferor is liable to pay under this Act (including, for greater certainty, an amount that the transferor is liable to pay under this section, regardless of whether the Minister has made an assessment under subsection (3) in respect of that amount) is deemed to have become payable in the calendar year of the transferor in which the property was transferred, if it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of the transfer of the property is to avoid the payment of a future amount payable under this Act by the transferor or transferee; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined for A in paragraph (1)‍(d) is deemed to be the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount otherwise determined for A in paragraph (1)‍(d) without reference to this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the property at the time of the transfer, and

is the fair market value, at its lowest at any time during the period beginning immediately prior to the transaction or series of transactions and ending immediately after the transaction or series of transactions, of the consideration given by the transferee for the transfer of the property (other than any part of the consideration that is in a form that is cancelled or extinguished during that period and for which no property that is neither cancelled nor extinguished during that period is substituted) provided that the consideration is held by the transferor at that time.

Section.Text: Subsection 83(11) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, if a notice or other communication in respect of a person, other than a notice or other communication that refers to the business number of a person, is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or a computer system or other similar device, the notice or other communication is presumed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that an electronic message is sent, to the electronic address most recently provided before that date by the person to the Minister for the purposes of this subsection, informing the person that a notice or other communication requiring the person’s immediate attention is available in the person’s secure electronic account. A notice or other communication is considered to be made available if it is posted by the Minister in the person’s secure electronic account and the person has authorized that notices or other communications may be made available in this manner and has not before that date revoked that authorization in a manner specified by the Minister.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Date electronic notice sent – business account
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, a notice or other communication in respect of a person that is made available in electronic format such that it can be read or perceived by a person or computer system or other similar device and that refers to the business number of a person is presumed to be sent to the person and received by the person on the date that it is posted by the Minister in the secure electronic account in respect of the business number of the person, unless the person has requested, at least 30 days before that date, in a manner specified by the Minister, that such notices or other communications be sent by mail.
The Underused Housing Tax Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 1:

Section.Text: For the purposes of the portion of the definition residential property in section 2 of the Act before paragraph (a), a particular residential condominium unit that is part of a building containing four or more residential condominium units is prescribed property if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person that is the owner of all or substantially all of the residential condominium units in the building is the owner of the particular residential condominium unit; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all or substantially all of those residential condominium units of which the person is the owner are held by the person for the purpose of providing individuals with continuous occupancy of a residential condominium unit as a place of residence or lodging for a period of at least one month.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on December 31, 2022.
Paragraphs 2(2)(a) and (b) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an area that is, as determined in the census for 2021 as published by Statistics Canada, neither within a census metropolitan area nor within a specified census agglomeration; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an area that is, as determined in the census for 2021 as published by Statistics Canada

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within a census metropolitan area or specified census agglomeration, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: not within a population centre.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 2(2)(a) and (b) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an area that is, as determined in the last census published by Statistics Canada before the calendar year, neither within a census metropolitan area nor within a specified census agglomeration; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an area that is, as determined in the last census published by Statistics Canada before the calendar year

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: within a census metropolitan area or specified census agglomeration, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: not within a population centre.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(3) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph 6(7)(m) of the Act, each of the following paragraphs sets out conditions that are prescribed conditions for a calendar year and for the person and the particular residential property referred to in subsection 6(7) of the Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the particular residential property is used as a place of residence or lodging by the person or the person’s spouse or common-law partner for at least 28 days during the calendar year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person, or another person that is related to the person, carries on business in Canada (in this paragraph referred to as the “operator”) and the particular residential property is held during the calendar year primarily to provide a place of residence or lodging to an individual at a location at which the individual is required to be in the performance of the individual’s duties as

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an officer — being a person holding an office — or an employee of the operator,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a contractor, or an employee of the contractor, engaged by the operator to render services at that location to the operator, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a subcontractor, or an employee of the subcontractor, engaged by a contractor referred to in subparagraph (ii) to render services at that location that are acquired by the contractor for the purpose of supplying services to the operator.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 2(3)(a) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (3), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is the case that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular residential property is used as a place of residence or lodging by the person or the person’s spouse or common-law partner for at least 28 days during the calendar year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the person indicates that no tax is payable in respect of the particular residential property under this paragraph and paragraph 6(7)(m) of the Act in the return filed under the Act by the person for the particular residential property and for the calendar year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: neither the person nor the person’s spouse or common-law partner indicates that no tax is payable in respect of any residential property other than the particular residential property under this paragraph and paragraph 6(7)(m) of the Act in a return filed under the Act by the person or the person’s spouse or common-law partner for the calendar year; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2022 calendar year.
Subsections (2) and (3) apply to the 2023 and subsequent calendar years.
Subsection (4) applies to the 2024 and subsequent calendar years.
The portion of the definition confidential information in subsection 107(1) of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: but does not include information that does not directly or indirectly reveal the identity of the person to whom it relates and, for the purposes of applying subsections (3), (13) and (15) to a representative of a government entity that is not an official, includes only the information described in paragraph 211(6)(j) of the Excise Act, 2001. (renseignement confidentiel)

Subsection.Text: Subsections 107(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure of confidential information — Excise Act, 2001
Subsection.Text: Subsections 211(6) and (6.1) of the Excise Act, 2001 apply in this Part, with any modifications that the circumstances require, as if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the references in those subsections to the Excise Act, 2001 were references to this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: confidential information for the purposes of this Part were confidential information for the purposes of the Excise Act, 2001;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an authorized person for the purposes of this Part were an authorized person for the purposes of the Excise Act, 2001; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a business number for the purposes of this Part were a business number for the purposes of the Excise Act, 2001.

Subsection.Text: An official may provide

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any confidential information to an official of the Department of the Environment solely for the purposes of Part 2 or the formulation or evaluation of greenhouse gas pollution pricing policy;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any confidential information relating to His Majesty in right of a province to an official of a department or agency of the Government of Canada, if His Majesty in right of the province or an agent of His Majesty in right of the province is not in compliance with this Part or has stated that it will not comply with this Part, solely for the purposes of the evaluation of, or the formulation of a response to, the non-compliance or statement; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any confidential information relating to an agent of His Majesty in right of a province to an official of a department or agency of the Government of Canada, if the agent or His Majesty in right of the province is not in compliance with this Part or has stated that it will not comply with this Part, solely for the purposes of the evaluation of, or the formulation of a response to, the non-compliance or statement.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 107(9)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a representative of the government entity was provided with that information under subsection (6); and

Subsection.Text: Section 107 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Public disclosure — province or agent non-compliance
Subsection.Text: The Minister may communicate or otherwise make available to the public, in any manner that the Minister considers appropriate, confidential information relating to a person that is His Majesty in right of a province or an agent of His Majesty in right of a province if the following conditions are satisfied:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the person is His Majesty in right of a province, it is the case that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person is not in compliance with this Part or has publicly stated that it will not comply with this Part, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an agent of His Majesty in right of the province is not in compliance with this Part or has publicly stated that it will not comply with this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the person is an agent of His Majesty in right of a province, it is the case that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the person is not in compliance with this Part or has publicly stated that it will not comply with this Part, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: His Majesty in right of the province is not in compliance with this Part or has publicly stated that it will not comply with this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the confidential information relates to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a registration status of, or the status of an application for registration by, the person under this Part,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a return that is filed, or required to be filed, by the person under this Part, including information that is contained in the return or that could reasonably be expected to be contained in the return,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount that is payable under this Part by or to the person, including a charge, rebate, net charge, penalty or amount of interest,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a projection or estimation of an amount that is, or that could reasonably be expected to be, payable under this Part by or to the person, including a charge, rebate, net charge, penalty or amount of interest,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount that is assessed under this Part in relation to the person,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the extent to which an amount referred to in any of subparagraphs (iii) to (v) has or has not been paid,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: an amount that is paid under this Part by or to the person,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a quantity of fuel or combustible waste that is delivered, imported, brought into a listed province, used or otherwise quantified for purposes of this Part,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: a projection or estimation of a quantity of fuel or combustible waste that could reasonably be expected to be delivered, imported, brought into a listed province, used or otherwise quantified for purposes of this Part,

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: a step that is, or is not, taken or contemplated by the Minister in the administration or enforcement of this Part relating to the person, including information relating to the collection of an amount, an assessment, an objection to an assessment, an audit or an appeal or legal proceeding,

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: a step that is or is not taken by the person in relation to its compliance or non-compliance with this Part, including information relating to the collection of an amount, an assessment, an objection to an assessment, an audit or an appeal or legal proceeding, or

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: a declaration or representation made by the person relating to anything set out in subparagraphs (i) to (xi).

Subsection.Text: Information relating to His Majesty in right of a province or an agent of His Majesty in right of a province that is communicated, or otherwise made available to the public, by the Minister under subsection (9.1) is deemed not to be confidential information for the purposes of this section and section 134.

Section.Text: Subsection 134(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person to whom confidential information has been provided for a particular purpose under subsection 107(6) or (7) and that for any other purpose knowingly uses, provides to any person, allows the provision to any person of, or allows any person access to, that information is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $5,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 12 months, or to both.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Budget Implementation Act, 2022, No. 1 (Extension of Prohibition on Purchase of Residential Property by Non-Canadians)

Section.Text: Subsection 237(2) of the Budget Implementation Act, 2022, No. 1 is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Section 236 comes into force on the fourth anniversary of the day on which section 235 comes into force.

Section.Text: Section 11 of the National Housing Act is replaced by the following:
Despite anything in this Act, the total of the outstanding insured amounts of all insured loans may not exceed the sum of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: eight hundred billion dollars, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any additional amounts authorized by Parliament under an appropriation Act or other Act of Parliament on or after the day on which the Budget Implementation Act, 2024, No. 1 receives royal assent.

Section.Text: Section 11.1 of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 4(b) of the Borrowing Authority Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total amount of money borrowed by way of the issue and sale of Canada Mortgage Bonds that are guaranteed by the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation, except if they are purchased by the Minister and are not resold, other than for the purpose of providing a source of temporary liquidity, and

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the COVID-19 Emergency Response Act.
If section 150 of this Act comes into force before subsection 47(2) of the other Act, then that subsection 47(2) and sections 49 and 50 of the other Act are repealed.
If section 49 of the other Act comes into force before section 151 of this Act, then that section 151 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

If section 49 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 151 of this Act, then subsection 47(2) and sections 49 and 50 of the other Act are deemed never to have come into force and are repealed.
The Minister of Families, Children and Social Development may enter into a bilateral agreement with the government of a province respecting a national program for providing food in schools.
Any amount payable for the fiscal year beginning on April 1, 2024 under such a bilateral agreement is payable out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
The maximum aggregate amount that may be paid out under subsection (2) in respect of all provinces is $70,100,000.

Section.Text: The heading before section 11.1 of the Canada Student Loans Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Loan Forgiveness — Underserved Rural or Remote Communities

Section.Text: Subsection 11.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may forgive an amount in respect of a guaranteed student loan to a borrower who meets the prescribed conditions and begins to work in an underserved rural or remote community as a

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: family physician;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: nurse;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: nurse practitioner;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: early childhood educator;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: dentist;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: dental hygienist;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: pharmacist;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: midwife;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: teacher;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: social worker;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: psychologist;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: personal support worker; or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: physiotherapist.

Section.Text: Paragraph 17(r) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: defining the words and expressions “dental hygienist”, “dentist”, “early childhood educator”, “family physician”, “full-time student”, “midwife”, “nurse”, “nurse practitioner”, “part-time student”, “personal support worker”, “pharmacist”, “physiotherapist”, “psychologist”, “responsible officer of a lender”, “social worker”, “teacher” and “underserved rural or remote community” for the purposes of this Act;

Section.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this Act, the words and expressions “borrower”, “consolidated student loan agreement”, “course”, “dental hygienist”, “dentist”, “early childhood educator”, “family income”, “family physician”, “financial assistance”, “full-time student”, “loan year”, “midwife”, “nurse”, “nurse practitioner”, “part-time student”, “period of studies”, “personal support worker”, “pharmacist”, “physiotherapist”, “post-secondary school level”, “program of studies”, “psychologist”, “severe permanent disability”, “social worker”, “student loan”, “student loan agreement”, “teacher” and “underserved rural or remote community” have the meanings assigned by the regulations.

Section.Text: The heading before section 9.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Loan Forgiveness — Underserved Rural or Remote Communities

Section.Text: Subsection 9.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may forgive an amount in respect of a student loan to a borrower who meets the prescribed conditions and begins to work in an underserved rural or remote community as a

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: family physician;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: nurse;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: nurse practitioner;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: early childhood educator;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: dentist;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: dental hygienist;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: pharmacist;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: midwife;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: teacher;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: social worker;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: psychologist;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: personal support worker; or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: physiotherapist.

Section.Text: This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The definition primary caregiver in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Education Savings Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: primary caregiver means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a beneficiary, or a child referred to in subsections 6(1.1) to (1.3) and section 12.2, who is a qualified dependant, the eligible individual in respect of the beneficiary or child; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a beneficiary, or a child referred to in subsections 6(1.1) to (1.3) and section 12.2, in respect of whom a special allowance is payable under the Children’s Special Allowances Act, the department, agency or institution that maintains the beneficiary or child. (responsable)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to this Act and the regulations, on application to the Minister, in the form and manner approved by the Minister, the Minister may, in respect of a beneficiary under a registered education savings plan who was born after 2003 and is less than 31 years of age at the time of the application, pay to a trustee of a trust governed by the plan a Canada Learning Bond for the benefit of the trust. The Canada Learning Bond is to be paid on any terms and conditions that the Minister may specify by agreement between the Minister and the trustee.
Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If the Minister determines that a child born after 2023 would be eligible to have a Canada Learning Bond paid in their respect if they were a beneficiary under a registered education savings plan, the child’s Social Insurance Number is provided to the Minister and the child is not, to the Minister’s knowledge, a beneficiary under such a plan, the Minister must notify the child’s primary caregiver — in the form and manner that the Minister considers appropriate — that the child is eligible to have a Canada Learning Bond paid in their respect and that the Minister will open a registered education savings plan in respect of the child in accordance with subsections (1.2) and (1.3) for the purpose of paying the Canada Learning Bond.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Opening of registered education savings plan by Minister
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1.3), if the Minister determines that a child born after 2023 would be eligible to have a Canada Learning Bond paid in their respect if they were a beneficiary and the Minister is provided with the child’s Social Insurance Number, the Minister must open a registered education savings plan in respect of the child

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the child is less than three years of age on the day the Minister determines that the child is eligible, no earlier than the day on which the child reaches the age of four or no earlier than any other day determined by the Minister; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the child is three years of age or more on the day the Minister determines that the child is eligible, no earlier than the day that is 365 days after the day on which the Minister determines that the child is eligible or no earlier than any other day determined by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must not open a registered education savings plan in respect of a child under subsection (1.2) if the child’s primary caregiver or the primary caregiver’s cohabiting spouse or common-law partner has communicated to the Minister, in the form and manner approved by the Minister, their refusal of the payment of a Canada Learning Bond in respect of the child or if the child is a beneficiary.

For greater certainty, an application may be made to the Minister under subsection (1) in respect of a person even if a refusal referred to in subsection (1.3) was communicated to the Minister in respect of that person.
Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.4):

If the Minister opens a registered education savings plan under subsection (1.2), subject to this Act and the regulations, the Minister may, in respect of the beneficiary under the plan, pay to a trustee of a trust governed by the plan a Canada Learning Bond for the benefit of the trust. The Canada Learning Bond is to be paid on any terms and conditions that the Minister may specify by agreement between the Minister and the trustee.
Subsection 6(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The amount of a Canada Learning Bond payable under subsection (1) in respect of a benefit year is to be paid to the trustee of a trust designated, in the form and manner approved by the Minister, by the primary caregiver of the beneficiary, the primary caregiver’s cohabiting spouse or common-law partner or, if the beneficiary is 18 years of age or more, the beneficiary.

Section.Text: The portion of section 7 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Neither a CES grant nor a Canada Learning Bond payable under subsection 6(1) may be paid in respect of a beneficiary under a registered education savings plan unless
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

A Canada Learning Bond payable under subsection 6(1.5) must not be paid in respect of a beneficiary under a registered education savings plan unless the beneficiary is resident in Canada immediately before the payment is made.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

Section.MarginalNote: Administration of registered education savings plans
Section.Text: The Minister may enter into an agreement with a person respecting the administration of a registered education savings plan opened under subsection 6(1.2) or any other registered education savings plan identified by the Minister. The agreement may provide for the payment of remuneration by the Minister to the person for the administration of the plan.
Section 9 of the Act is replaced by the following:
All amounts payable by the Minister under this Act, the regulations or an agreement entered into under section 7.1 or 12 must be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 12.1:

The Minister may collect the Social Insurance Number of any person who, as determined by the Minister, would be eligible to have a Canada Learning Bond paid in their respect if they were a beneficiary and, if the person is a child, the Social Insurance Number of the child’s primary caregiver and the primary caregiver’s cohabiting spouse or common-law partner.

Subsection.Text: Section 13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the notice referred to in subsection 6(1.1), including providing for the maximum age of a child in respect of whom a notice is to be provided under that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (c.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the opening of registered education savings plans by the Minister under subsection 6(1.2), including providing for the maximum age of a child in respect of whom such a plan may be opened under that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (c.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the refusal referred to in subsection 6(1.3);

Paragraph.Label: (c.4)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the administration of the registered education savings plans referred to in section 7.1;

Paragraph.Label: (c.5)
Paragraph.Text: respecting agreements made under section 7.1, including specifying terms and conditions to be included in such agreements;

Subsection.Text: Section 13 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c.3):

Paragraph.Label: (c.31)
Paragraph.Text: respecting payments under subsection 6(1.5);

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendments to the Income Tax Act

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition education savings plan in subsection 146.1(1) of the Income Tax Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iv):

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the Minister designated for the purposes of the Canada Education Savings Act, and

Subsection.Text: The definition subscriber in subsection 146.1(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister designated for the purposes of the Canada Education Savings Act, if that Minister entered into the plan with a promoter

Subsection.Text: Section 162, subsection 163(2), sections 166 to 168, subsection 169(1) and section 170 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsections 163(1), (3) and (4), sections 164 and 165 and subsection 169(2) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day must be later than the day fixed under subsection (1) and after March 31, 2028.

MarginalNote.HistoricalNote: R.S., c. B-7; R.S., c. 24 (1st Supp.), s. 3

TitleText.XRefExternal: Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act

Section.Text: Paragraphs 8.3(5)(a) and (b) of the Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fifteen billion dollars in respect of any particular foreign state; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: twenty-two billion dollars in respect of all foreign states.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 7
Heading.TitleText: Measures Relating to Modernizing International Financial Institutions

MarginalNote.HistoricalNote: R.S., c. B-7; R.S., c. 24 (1st Supp.), s. 3

TitleText.XRefExternal: Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister of Finance may provide for payment out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the International Monetary Fund in the manner and at the times provided for by the Agreement set out in Schedule I of a sum or sums of money, not exceeding in the whole an amount equivalent to the subscriptions required from or permitted to be made by Canada, namely, sixteen billion, five hundred thirty-five million, nine hundred thousand Special Drawing Rights.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(c) of the English version of the International Development (Financial Institutions) Assistance Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase on behalf of His Majesty in right of Canada of shares of the institution;

Subsection.Text: Section 3 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: with the written concurrence of the Minister of Finance, the issuance of guarantees; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: with the written concurrence of the Minister of Finance, any other manner that the Minister of Foreign Affairs considers appropriate.

TitleText.XRefExternal: European Bank for Reconstruction and Development Agreement Act

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 6(2) of the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development Agreement Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Minister may provide financial assistance to the Bank by way of
Subsection 6(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase of shares on behalf of His Majesty in right of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the issuance of guarantees; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any other manner that the Minister considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may make payments out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund for the purposes of subsection (2) in an aggregate amount not exceeding, in any given period, the amount specified for that purpose in respect of that period in an appropriation by Parliament.

Section.Text: The International Financial Assistance Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:
Any foreign exchange loss in respect of any transaction entered into in relation to a program referred to in section 3, 4 or 5 must be charged to the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Subsection.Text: The competent minister may, in relation to a program referred to in section 4 or 5, make payments not exceeding $720,000,000 in the aggregate, or any greater amount that is specified in an appropriation Act, to Development Finance Institute Canada.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Payments out of Consolidated Revenue Fund
Subsection.Text: Any amount payable under subsection (1) may be paid by the competent minister out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, at the times and in the manner that the minister considers appropriate.
Development Finance Institute Canada must maintain a separate account of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts received under subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts received by way of receipts and recoveries, and all disbursements made, in connection with all transactions involving the amounts received under subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: The competent minister may require Development Finance Institute Canada to pay to the Receiver General for credit to the Consolidated Revenue Fund any amount referred to in subsection (3) at the times and in the manner that the minister considers appropriate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definition of Development Finance Institute Canada
Subsection.Text: In this section, Development Finance Institute Canada means Development Finance Institute Canada (DFIC) Inc., a corporation incorporated under the Canada Business Corporation Act, or any successor to that corporation.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8:

The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs and the Minister for International Development and with the concurrence of the Minister of Finance, make regulations for the purposes of section 7.2.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 24(1) of the Export Development Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (2), in respect of transactions entered into under section 23, the total of the following liabilities and obligations must not at any time exceed $100,000,000,000:
Subsections 24(1.1) and (1.2) of the Act are repealed.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 10
Heading.TitleText: Financial Administration Act (Exemption Related to Certain Crown Corporations)

Section.Text: Subsection 85(2) of the Financial Administration Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: by or on behalf of the Communications Security Establishment for the purpose of performing its functions under the Communications Security Establishment Act; or

Section.Text: Section 159 of the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Any bank or other financial institution of a class prescribed by regulations that accepts for deposit an instrument or instruction for payment described in subsection (2) must disclose any information prescribed by regulations made under subsection 160(2) in any statement of account or other transaction record that is provided to the holder of the account receiving the deposit.

Section.Text: Section 160 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 160(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Classes of financial institutions and disclosure of information
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations prescribing classes of financial institutions and any information disclosure requirements referred to in subsection 159(4).

Paragraph 24.1(1)(a) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by replacing the portion of subparagraph (v) before the formula with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2017, the product, rounded to the nearest thousand, obtained by multiplying the cash contribution determined under this subparagraph for the immediately preceding fiscal year by the greater of 1.03 and the amount determined by the formula

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 24.1(1)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (v) and by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the average of the annual rates of growth of the nominal gross domestic product of Canada for the calendar year that ends during the fiscal year in question and for the two previous calendar years, as determined by the Minister not later than three months before the beginning of that fiscal year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: for each fiscal year that begins after March 31, 2029, in respect of a qualifying province, the product, rounded to the nearest thousand, determined by the formula

is the greater of 1.03 and the amount determined by the formula

is the average of the annual rates of growth of the nominal gross domestic product of Canada for the calendar year that ends during the fiscal year in question and for the two previous calendar years, as determined by the Minister not later than three months before the beginning of that fiscal year; and

Subsection.Text: Section 24.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

For the purpose of this Part, a province is a qualifying province if the federal Minister of Health has confirmed in writing to the Minister before December 1, 2024 that the province has taken steps to implement certain measures respecting the collection, sharing and use of certain health information in accordance with the plan entitled Working together to improve health care for Canadians, announced on February 7, 2023.

Section.Text: Section 24.21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Provincial share — fiscal years 2014–2015 and later

Subsection.Text: The cash contribution determined under subparagraphs 24.1(1)(a)(iv) or (v), as the case may be, that may be provided to a province for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2014 is the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the cash contribution determined under the applicable subparagraph for that fiscal year;
is the population of that province for that fiscal year; and
is the total of the population of all provinces for that fiscal year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provincial share (qualifying province) — fiscal years 2024–2025 and later
Subsection.Text: The cash contribution determined under subparagraph 24.1(1)(a)(vi), (vii) or (viii), as the case may be, that may be provided to a qualifying province for each fiscal year beginning after March 31, 2024 is the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the cash contribution determined under the applicable subparagraph for that fiscal year;
is the population of that province for that fiscal year; and
is the total of the population of all provinces for that fiscal year.

Section.Text: The Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985 is amended by adding the following after section 40:

Within a reasonable time after the end of each calendar year, the Superintendent shall, in the form and manner that may be prescribed, publish the prescribed information that relates to the investments of prescribed pension plans.
The Pooled Registered Pension Plans Act is amended by adding the following after section 43:

Subsection.Text: An administrator of a pooled registered pension plan must provide each person who becomes a member of the plan, other than under section 39 or 40, with a written notice that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: informs the member of their right to terminate their membership in the plan by notifying the administrator; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: includes the prescribed information.

Subsection.Text: The administrator must provide the notice

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a survivor of a deceased member, as soon as feasible after the day on which the survivor becomes a member; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, no later than the day on which the person becomes a member.

Section.Text: Sections 184 and 185 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition child in subsection 42(1) of the Canada Pension Plan is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual in respect of whom, either legally or in fact, the contributor had, or immediately before the individual reached 21 years of age did have, parenting time or decision-making responsibility,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition dependent child in subsection 42(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is 18 or more years of age but less than 25 years of age and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is in full-time attendance at a school or university as defined by regulation, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is in part-time attendance at a school or university as defined by regulation, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 42(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: decision-making responsibility has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Divorce Act; (responsabilités décisionnelles)

parenting time means the time that a child spends in the care of a person who is a parent of the child, who stands in the place of a parent or who intends to stand in the place of a parent, whether or not the child is physically with that person during that entire time; (temps parental)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 44.1:

Despite paragraph 44(1)(d), a survivor’s pension is not payable to an individual as a result of a contributor’s death if, on or after January 1, 2025, a division of unadjusted pensionable earnings has taken place under paragraph 55.1(1)(b) between the contributor and that individual unless, at the time of the contributor’s death, that individual had been cohabiting with the contributor in a new conjugal relationship for a continuous period of at least one year.
Section 57 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.1):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a death benefit payable to the estate or succession of a contributor is a lump sum amount equal to $5,000 if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contributor’s death occurs after December 31, 2024;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a death benefit is payable to the contributor’s estate or succession under paragraph 44(1)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contributor has not received a retirement pension or a disability pension under this Act or under a provincial pension plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: no survivor’s pension is payable under paragraph 44(1)(d) as a result of the contributor’s death.

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 59 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Except in the case of a dependent child described in subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition dependent child in subsection 42(1), a disabled contributor’s child’s benefit payable to the child of a disabled contributor and an orphan’s benefit payable to the orphan of a contributor, is a basic monthly amount consisting of
Section 59 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

In the case of a dependent child described in subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition dependent child in subsection 42(1), a disabled contributor’s child’s benefit payable to the child of a disabled contributor and an orphan’s benefit payable to the orphan of a contributor, is a basic monthly amount equal to 50% of the amount that would otherwise be calculated under paragraph (1)(c).
Subsection 60(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a disabled contributor’s child’s benefit would, if the application had been approved, have been payable to a child of a disabled contributor on application made prior to the death of the child or an orphan’s benefit would, if the application had been approved, have been payable to an orphan of a contributor on application made prior to the death of the orphan and the child or orphan dies after December 31, 1977, not having reached 18 years of age, and no application has been made at the time of the death of the child or orphan, an application may be made within one year after the death by the person or agency having decision-making responsibility in respect of the child or orphan at the time of the death or, if there is at that time no such person or agency, by any person or agency that the Minister may direct.
Paragraph 60(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the date of the death of a child or an orphan referred to in subsection (3) if the person or agency having decision-making responsibility in respect of the child or orphan did not make an application prior to the death of the child or orphan.

Subsection.Text: Section 60 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (11):

If an application for a disability pension or a post-retirement disability benefit is deemed, under subsection (8) or (9), to have been made in a month that precedes the month in which it was actually made, the Minister may also deem the application for a disabled contributor’s child’s benefit to have been made on behalf of the contributor’s child in that same month.

Section.Text: Section 73 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), a survivor’s pension shall cease with the payment for the month of the effective date of the approval of a division of unadjusted pensionable earnings between the survivor and the contributor under paragraph 55.1(1)(b) unless, at the time of the contributor’s death, the survivor had been cohabiting with the contributor in a new conjugal relationship for a continuous period of at least one year.

Section.Text: Section 75 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a disabled contributor’s child’s benefit is payable to a child of a disabled contributor or an orphan’s benefit is payable to an orphan of a contributor and the child or orphan has not reached 18 years of age, payment of the benefit must be made to the person or agency having decision-making responsibility for the child or orphan, or, if there is no person or agency having decision-making responsibility for the child or orphan, to any person or agency that the Minister may direct.
For the purposes of this Part, in the absence of any evidence to the contrary, the following person is presumed to have decision-making responsibility for the child or orphan, as the case may be:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contributor, in relation to a disabled contributor’s child, except if the contributor has less than 20% of the parenting time in respect of the child; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the survivor, if any, of the contributor, in relation to an orphan, except if the survivor has less than 20% of the parenting time in respect of the orphan.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 76(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contributor’s disability pension or post-retirement disability benefit ceases to be payable for any reason other than that the contributor has reached 65 years of age;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 76(1)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the disabled contributor ceases to have parenting time or decision-making responsibility in respect of the child, if the child is a child as defined in subsection 42(1) by reason of the disabled contributor having had that parenting time or decision-making responsibility.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Canada Pension Plan Regulations

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 52(i)(iii) and (iv) of the Canada Pension Plan Regulations are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: was a child, in respect of whom, either legally or in fact, the contributor had parenting time or decision-making responsibility,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: is a child, in respect of whom, the disabled contributor, the survivor of the contributor or another person or agency had decision-making responsibility,

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application — subsection 114(2) of Canada Pension Plan

Subsection.Text: Subsection 114(2) of the Canada Pension Plan does not apply in respect of the amendments to that Act contained in this Division.

This Division, other than subsections 187(1) and (3), sections 191 and 193, subsection 194(2) and section 195, comes into force, in accordance with subsection 114(4) of the Canada Pension Plan, on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act

Section.Text: The Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.1:

Subsection.Text: The Board shall, at the request of the Minister, pay into the Consolidated Revenue Fund

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is required for the payment of benefits under subsection 44.2(6) of the Public Service Superannuation Act, subsection 55.2(6) of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act or regulations made under section 59.1 of that Act, or subsection 29.2(6) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is determined under paragraph 44.4(2)(b) of the Public Service Superannuation Act, paragraph 55.4(2)(b) of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act or paragraph 29.4(2)(b) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that is required for the payment of the costs determined under section 44.5 of the Public Service Superannuation Act, section 55.5 of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act or section 29.5 of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act or the costs for the administration of a fund established by regulations made under section 59.1 of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act.

Subsection.Text: Before requesting the payment of an amount referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (c), the Minister shall consult

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of National Defence if the amount is in relation to the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness if the amount is in relation to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act.

Section.Text: The Consumer-Driven Banking Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to establish a consumer-driven banking framework

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Consumer-Driven Banking Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Agency means the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada established under section 3 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act. (Agence)
derived data means data about a consumer, product or service that has been enhanced by a participating entity to significantly increase its usefulness or commercial value. (données dérivées)
entity means a corporation, trust, partnership, fund or an unincorporated association or organization. (entité)
Minister means the Minister of Finance. (ministre)

Senior Deputy Commissioner means the Senior Deputy Commissioner for Consumer-Driven Banking appointed under subsection 7.2(1) of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act. (commissaire adjoint principal)

Section.Text: The purpose of this Act is to establish a framework within which consumers, including small businesses, can direct that their data be shared among participating entities of their choice and to ensure that the sharing of data among participating entities is safe and secure.

Subsection.Text: This Act applies in respect of data that relates to the following products and services, including data that is provided by a consumer and data that is provided for in the regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: deposit accounts;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: registered and non-registered investment accounts;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: payment products, including credit cards and prepaid payment products;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: lines of credit, mortgages or hypothecs and other kinds of loans; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: other products or services provided for in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: This Act does not apply in respect of derived data.

Section.Text: Data that is shared between participating entities in accordance with this Act is to be shared in a manner that does not enable the participating entity that receives the data to edit the data on servers that are used by the participating entity that provides the data.

Nothing in this Act affects any restriction imposed under the Bank Act on banks with respect to the sharing of information about a consumer with an insurance company, agent or broker for the business of insurance.
The Agency must maintain a public registry of participating entities that contains information respecting each participating entity.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, designate a body to be the technical standards body that is responsible for establishing the technical standards that are to be used for the sharing of data by participating entities in accordance with this Act.
In designating the technical standards body, the Minister must take into account the following principles:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the need to ensure the safe, secure and efficient sharing of data among participating entities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fairness, accessibility, transparency and good governance;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other principle that the Minister considers relevant; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other principle provided for in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must publish the order in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: The Minister must review the designation every three years.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, revoke the designation, including in the following circumstances:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Senior Deputy Commissioner advises the Minister to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is of the opinion that the designation is no longer consistent with the principles referred to in subsection 8(2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is of the opinion that the designation poses a risk to national security; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister is of the opinion that the designation poses a risk to the integrity or security of the financial system in Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must publish the order in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection 8(1) or 10(1).
The technical standards body that is designated must submit an annual report to the Senior Deputy Commissioner in accordance with the regulations.

The technical standards body that is designated must notify the Senior Deputy Commissioner of any change that has a significant impact on the technical standards body or the technical standards, including any change to the operation of the technical standards or the governance, composition or decision-making of the technical standards body, as soon as feasible but no later than the seventh day after the day on which the change takes effect.
An individual or entity, other than a participating entity, must not

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use the term “participating entity” or a variation, abbreviation or equivalent of that term, or any words, name or designation — in any language — in a manner that leads to a reasonable belief that the individual or entity is a participating entity for the purposes of this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: represent themselves, in any way or by any means, to be a participating entity for the purposes of this Act.

Section.Text: An individual or entity must not knowingly provide false or misleading information in relation to their participation under this Act.
Every individual or entity who contravenes section 14 or 15 is guilty of an offence and is liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than five years, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity, to a fine of not more than $5,000,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $100,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than one year, or to both, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of an entity, to a fine of not more than $500,000.

Subsection.Text: If an individual or entity is convicted of an offence under this Act, the court may, in addition to any punishment that it may otherwise impose, order the individual or entity to comply with the provisions of this Act or the regulations in respect of which the individual or entity was convicted.

If an individual or entity is convicted of an offence under this Act, the court may, if it is satisfied that as a result of the commission of the offence the convicted individual or entity acquired any monetary benefits or that monetary benefits accrued to the convicted individual or entity or the individual’s spouse, common-law partner or other dependant, order the convicted individual or entity to pay, despite the maximum amount of any fine that may otherwise be imposed under this Act, an additional fine in an amount equal to three times the court’s estimation of the amount of those monetary benefits.

Section.Text: If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any director, any officer, any agent or mandatary or any principal officer of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and is liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided for an individual in respect of the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.

Subsection.Text: Proceedings by way of summary conviction in respect of an offence under this Act may be commenced at any time within, but not later than, two years after the day on which the subject matter of the proceedings became known to the Senior Deputy Commissioner.

A document appearing to have been issued by the Senior Deputy Commissioner, certifying the day on which the subject matter of any proceedings became known to the Senior Deputy Commissioner, is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it and is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of the matter asserted in it.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: providing for data for the purposes of subsection 4(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: providing for products and services for the purposes of paragraph 4(1)(e);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for principles for the purposes of paragraph 8(2)(d); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the annual report referred to in section 12, including the information that is to be included in the report and the form and manner in which, and the time within which, it is to be submitted.

Section.Text: Sections 4 to 7, 12 and 13 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendments to the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: participating entity means a participating entity under the Consumer-Driven Banking Act. (entité participante)
